sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Compare before and after to see what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

# Check segmentation differences
before_seg = before.segmentation
after_seg = after.segmentation

print("Before nodes:")
for n in before_seg['nodes']:
    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']}")

print("\nAfter nodes:")
for n in after_seg['nodes']:
    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check the pink bar specifically
before_pink = [n for n in before_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'P']
after_pink = [n for n in after_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'P']
print(f"\nBefore pink: {[(n['pixels'], n['boundary'][:3]) for n in before_pink]}")
print(f"After pink: {[(n['pixels'], n['boundary'][:3]) for n in after_pink]}")

# Check what changed in the ascii around the click area
before_lines = before.ascii.split('\n')
after_lines = after.ascii.split('\n')
for r in range(64):
    if before_lines[r] != after_lines[r]:
        print(f"\nRow {r} changed:")
        print(f"  before: {before_lines[r]}")
        print(f"  after:  {after_lines[r]}")
        # Find the diff position
        for c in range(64):
            if before_lines[r][c] != after_lines[r][c]:
                print(f"  Diff at col {c}: '{before_lines[r][c]}' -> '{after_lines[r][c]}'")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
Interesting! The change was:
1. The pink bar at row 0 lost one pixel (64 -> 63) - the rightmost pixel changed from P to c (charcoal)
2. A new charcoal node (id=1, 1 pixel) appeared at position [0, 63]
So clicking on the yellow gap caused the pink timer bar to shrink by 1 pixel on the right side, changing it to charcoal. This confirms the pink bar is a timer/step counter that decreases with each action.

- A charcoal + yellow cluster below
